title: Privacy Policy
text:
The controller responsible for data processing on this website is:
Dr. Nils Roemer
Billhorner Deich 88
20539 Hamburg
Germany
Email: nils.roemer@planningio.com
This is a personal website. It does not use web analytics, tracking, cookies, or embedded third-party content (such as fonts, videos, or maps loaded from external servers). To exercise your rights or for any questions about data processing, please contact me using the details above.



This website is hosted on GitHub Pages, a service provided by GitHub, Inc., 88 Colin P. Kelly Jr. Street, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA. When you visit this website, GitHub technically requires certain connection data to deliver the pages to your device. This includes your IP address, the date and time of your request, the pages accessed, and general browser and operating system information. This data is processed transiently in server logs to deliver the website and to ensure its security and stability; it is not merged with other data sources by me or used to identify individual visitors.
Because GitHub, Inc. is based in the USA, your data may be transferred to a third country. GitHub is certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework, which the European Commission has recognised as providing an adequate level of data protection (adequacy decision of 10 July 2023). This forms the basis for the transfer.
Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in providing and securing this website).



This website does not set cookies and does not use any tracking, analytics, or marketing tools. Fonts and all other assets are served directly from this website, so no data is transmitted to third parties beyond the hosting provider described in Section 2 when you view a page. No information is stored on or read from your device, so no consent under § 25 TDDDG is required.



Under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

Right to access (Art. 15 GDPR)
Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR)
Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR)
Right to restrict processing (Art. 18 GDPR)
Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR)
Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR)

To exercise any of these rights, please contact me as the controller using the details in Section 1. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular in the EU member state of your habitual residence, place of work, or the place of the alleged infringement.



I may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. The current version is always the one published on this page. I encourage you to review this policy periodically.

title: Nils Roemer
text:
Photo © Xenia Bluhm



I am an entrepreneur, researcher, and lecturer based in Hamburg, Germany.
I hold a degree in Business Administration with a focus on Operations and Supply Chain Management (2018) and a PhD in Operations Research and Mathematical Optimization (2022). Since 2023, I am Co-Founder and Managing Director of Planningio GmbH, a Hamburg-based software and consulting company specializing in mathematical optimization.
Our projects span automated production planning for the beverage industry, crowd management, warehouse simulation, and industrial digitalization.
I teach to inspire students to think analytically and to bring quantitative methods to life through real-world applications.

 LinkedIn Email

title: Nils Roemer
text:
I am an entrepreneur, researcher, and lecturer based in Hamburg, Germany.
I hold a degree in Business Administration with a focus on Operations and Supply Chain Management (2018) and a PhD in Operations Research and Mathematical Optimization (2022). Since 2023, I am Co-Founder and Managing Director of Planningio GmbH, a Hamburg-based software and consulting company specializing in mathematical optimization.
Our projects span automated production planning for the beverage industry, crowd management, warehouse simulation, and industrial digitalization.
I teach to inspire students to think analytically and to bring quantitative methods to life through real-world applications.

 LinkedIn Email

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Co-Founder & Managing Director — Planningio GmbH, Hamburg · since 2023
Planningio is a Hamburg-based consulting boutique and software company specializing in mathematical optimization, simulation, and digital twins (planningio.com). We combine analysis, modeling, and implementation from a single source, with projects ranging from one-off analyses to production-grade software.

Clients across industry, logistics, infrastructure, and major events, from automated production planning in the beverage industry to crowd management for millions of pilgrims in Makkah

Lecturer — Kühne Logistics University, Hamburg · since 2024

English-language course based on the book “Algorithms to Live By”, focusing on algorithms and optimization in everyday and business decisions
Developed a hands-on Python format introducing students to data-driven decision models

Lecturer — Universität Hamburg · 2023

Interdisciplinary course on entrepreneurship in operations and supply chain management
Strong involvement of industry partners to give students real-world founding experience

Research Associate — Universität Hamburg · 2018 – 2022

Teaching in production and logistics (B.Sc.) and supply chain management (M.Sc.); supervision of seminar, bachelor’s, and master’s theses on quantitative methods
Research and publications in business analytics

Intern, Supply Chain Management — Wallenius Wilhelmsen, Bremen · 2016

Norwegian-Swedish shipping company specializing in rolling cargo, in particular global vehicle logistics
Contributed to implementing a factory-to-dealer approach for BMW to efficiently manage global vehicle supply chains




Dr. rer. oec. (summa cum laude) — Universität Hamburg · 2018 – 2022
Dissertation: “Choice-based analytics with applications in supply chain contracting and carbon emission reduction initiatives”
M.Sc. Business Administration — Universität Hamburg · 2015 – 2018
B.Sc. Business Administration — Universität Hamburg · 2012 – 2015






Roemer, N., Müller, S., & Voigt, G. (2023). A choice-based optimization approach for contracting in supply chains. European Journal of Operational Research, 305(1), 271–286. doi.org/10.1016/j.ejor.2022.05.052
Roemer, N., Souza, G. C., Tröster, C., & Voigt, G. (2023). Offset or reduce: How should firms implement carbon footprint reduction initiatives? Production and Operations Management, 32(9), 2940–2955. doi.org/10.1111/poms.14017





Roemer, N. Strategic product returns and the optimization of shipping fee policies in online retailing. Working paper.
Roemer, N., & Voigt, G. Carbon emission reduction preferences and hypothetical bias in experiments on sustainability. Working paper.






Professor Herbert Jacob Prize, Universitäts-Gesellschaft Hamburg (2024), biennial dissertation prize for business administration research with high relevance for both academia and practice
Gründungspreis, Digital Innovations Competition, German Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Climate Action (2023), Planningio recognized as one of Germany’s most innovative start-ups
Best Master’s Graduate, Universität Hamburg (2018)





Reviewer for OR Spectrum and Transportation Research
Juror for the Hamburg Teaching Award and the KREATI case study competition (Verband Deutscher Wirtschaftsingenieure e.V.)




Core: Mathematical optimization & operations research · Statistics & data science · Supply chain management & logistics · Entrepreneurship & business leadership
Tools: Python, R · GAMS, MATLAB, CPLEX · LaTeX, Quarto



German (native) · English (fluent) · French (basic)

Dr. Nils Roemer
Billhorner Deich 88
20539 Hamburg
Germany
Email: nils.roemer@planningio.com

title: Teaching
text:
I teach quantitative and computational methods with a focus on making algorithmic thinking accessible to business students — connecting optimization and data-driven decision-making to practical, everyday problems.


Kühne Logistics University · ongoing
An English-language course based on the book Algorithms to Live By. It introduces business students to algorithmic decision-making — from optimal stopping and scheduling to caching and randomness — alongside a hands-on introduction to Python, with no prior programming experience required.
Course website →
